finding: Publish blockers are revealed one at a time — fix the SEO title and a new blocker appears, fix that and another appears ("Fix content 1 · Destination URL"). It is whack-a-mole with no full list shown up front.

evidence.quote: status chip: draft · Fix SEO title, Publish disabled
readiness blockers that are revealed ONE AT A TIME in the status chip

persona_rationale: I'd think I was almost done, then another problem appears out of nowhere. After three surprise blockers I'd give up — apps I use never hide the finish line from me.

suggested_fix.current: Status chip 'draft · Fix SEO title' showing one blocker at a time
suggested_fix.proposed: Show ALL blockers at once as a checklist: 'Before you can publish: ☐ Add a headline ☐ Add a button label ☐ Add a button link'

finding: The chip says 'Fix SEO title' but the SEO title field's own placeholder says 'Leave blank to use page headline' — telling me to fix something it also says is optional.

evidence.quote: chip 'Fix SEO title' with field placeholder 'Leave blank to use page headline'
Leave blank to use page headline

persona_rationale: Two messages fighting each other on the same screen. I'd fill the box, it'd stay angry, and I'd have no idea why.

suggested_fix.current: Chip 'Fix SEO title' while the field is marked optional
suggested_fix.proposed: Chip 'Add your keyword to the page title or headline', and stop demanding the SEO title when the field says it can be left blank

finding: 'Fix content 1 · Destination URL' points at a field that is not on the page — it is hidden inside Block actions → Edit settings modal — and the error gives no hint where to go.

evidence.quote: the destination field is NOT on the canvas; it lives in Block actions → Edit settings modal... The blocker text gives no hint where the field is
CTA destination URL only in block settings modal

persona_rationale: I hunted everywhere for 'Destination URL' and it didn't exist on screen. A 16-year-old does not know to dig into a hidden menu. Dead stop.

suggested_fix.current: 'Fix content 1 · Destination URL' with no location hint
suggested_fix.proposed: 'Your button needs a link — tap the button, then Edit settings to add where it goes' (or surface the field inline on the block)

finding: After Confirm publish, a 'Changes published.' toast appears but the yellow confirm card stays open asking to publish again, so I can't tell if it actually worked.

evidence.quote: 'Changes published.' toast appears BUT the confirm card remains open asking to publish again
Confirm publish card with 'This will make this draft visible at /resources/...' still showing

persona_rationale: Did it post or not? On Insta when I post, the screen changes and I see my post. Here it just sits there asking again. Confusing and a little scary.

suggested_fix.current: Confirm card stays open after success with 'This will replace the current live version'
suggested_fix.proposed: Close the confirm card on success and show 'Published! View your live page →'

finding: Setting a schedule date/time and saving re-renders the field empty with no 'Scheduled for...' confirmation anywhere — no way to know it worked.

evidence.quote: the field re-rendered EMPTY and no 'Scheduled for ...' status appeared anywhere in the editor
Scheduled publish field with 11/06/2026, 12:00 PM before save

persona_rationale: If I schedule a TikTok it shows me 'scheduled for...' right there. A blank box tells me nothing. I'd assume it broke.

suggested_fix.current: Schedule field blanks out silently after save
suggested_fix.proposed: Show a clear banner after saving: 'Scheduled to go live Jun 11, 12:00 PM'

finding: The schedule field says 'Uses Pacific Time (America/Los_Angeles)' for an Australian business — the wrong timezone is shown to everyone scheduling a page.

evidence.quote: Uses Pacific Time (America/Los_Angeles). Leave blank unless this page should publish later.
Label says 'Uses Pacific Time (America/Los_Angeles)' — for an Australian business

persona_rationale: Why is an Aussie business on California time? I'd schedule something for noon and it'd post at like 5am. That's a real mess-up.

suggested_fix.proposed: Use the business's actual timezone (Australia), e.g. 'Goes live at this time, Adelaide time'

finding: Drafts have no undo before the first publish — 'Revisions appear after publishing, library refreshes, or draft restores.' If I wreck my draft, it's gone.

evidence.quote: revisions do NOT exist while drafting... a user who mangles a draft before first publish has no undo at the page level
Empty revision state before publish

persona_rationale: Every app I use has undo / 'recently deleted.' Knowing one wrong move can't be taken back makes me afraid to even touch anything.

suggested_fix.current: Empty state 'Revisions appear after publishing, library refreshes, or draft restores.'
suggested_fix.proposed: Add draft autosave history so there is always an undo point, even before first publish

finding: Created redirects have no delete or edit control — a typo is permanent from this screen.

evidence.quote: the redirects table offers no edit or delete control — mistakes are permanent from this UI
redirects table with OLD PATH/DESTINATION/STATUS/SOURCE/CREATED columns and no actions column

persona_rationale: If I typo the address there's no backspace. Nothing I use traps me with a permanent mistake like that.

suggested_fix.current: Redirects table has no per-row controls
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a delete (and edit) action to each redirect row

finding: '301', '302', '307', '308' status codes and the word 'Redirects' are pure dev/boomer jargon with no plain explanation.

evidence.quote: Permanent move (301) Temporary move (302) Temporary redirect (307) Permanent redirect (308)

persona_rationale: I have literally no idea what 307 vs 308 is and I'm not going to Google it. Just give me 'moved for good' or 'moved for now.'

suggested_fix.proposed: Default to 'Permanent move' with the number hidden, plus a one-line 'Use this when a page changed address'

finding: The new-page canvas is all grey ghost text ('Hero headline', 'Hero body copy', 'CTA label') with nothing saying these are required or where to start typing.

evidence.quote: /placeholder: Hero headline ... /placeholder: Hero body copy
ghost placeholder text in hero block

persona_rationale: It just looks like a half-loaded page. I didn't know the grey words were tappable or that I had to fill them before posting.

suggested_fix.proposed: Add a starter hint 'Tap the grey text to write your headline' or pre-fill an example

finding: The right-side panel is a wall of stacked sections — Readiness, Page title, Slug, Target keyword, SEO title, Meta description, Advanced SEO, Search Preview, Action Items, Governance comments — far too much at once.

evidence.quote: full editor showing stacked SEO/Action Items/Governance panels
Action Items ... Builder support ... Governance comments

persona_rationale: This is the definition of 'boomer software' — fifty options screaming at me. I don't know what 80% of it does and I freeze.

suggested_fix.current: Every section expanded and stacked by default
suggested_fix.proposed: Show only Title + Publish first, collapse advanced sections behind a 'More options' toggle

finding: Coloured dots in the 'Readiness' and 'Status' columns have no visible text labels — I can't tell what red/yellow/blue/green mean without hovering.

evidence.quote: colour-only dots under Readiness and Status columns
cell 'SEO readiness: Needs work': button 'SEO readiness: Needs work'

persona_rationale: Colours with no words is a guessing game. Is red bad? Is blue good? I shouldn't have to mouse over to find out.

suggested_fix.proposed: Add a short text label next to each dot ('Needs work', 'Draft', 'Published')

finding: The header 'SEO PAGE BUILDER' and the field word 'Slug' are jargon a teen won't understand.

evidence.quote: SEO PAGE BUILDER
Slug

persona_rationale: 'Slug' sounds like a bug from my garden, not a web thing. SEO means nothing to me. Plain words please.

suggested_fix.proposed: 'Slug' → 'Page web address' with an example; add '(this helps Google find your page)' to first-time SEO mentions

finding: After publishing there is no obvious 'Open live page' link in the default panel — the live URL had to be typed by hand.

evidence.quote: No 'Open live page' link was visible after publishing in the default panel state — the live URL had to be typed manually

persona_rationale: The payoff of posting is seeing it live. Hiding that link removes the one satisfying moment and makes me think it didn't work.

suggested_fix.current: No live-page link surfaced after publish
suggested_fix.proposed: Show a 'View your live page →' button immediately after publishing

finding: A floating black 'N' circle button overlaps the 'Sign out' button in the bottom-left corner, looking glitchy.

evidence.quote: black 'N' circle widget overlapping 'Sign out' label
same 'N' widget overlap on mobile

persona_rationale: It looks broken/glitchy, like two things crashed into each other. Sloppy — and on a real app that screams 'don't trust me.'

suggested_fix.current: Floating widget sits on top of the 'Sign out' label
suggested_fix.proposed: Reposition the floating widget so it never covers the sign-out control

finding: Typing a title then leaving the unsaved new-page editor navigates away with no 'unsaved changes' warning — typed work is silently lost.

evidence.quote: navigated straight to /admin/pages with NO unsaved-changes warning — typed title silently lost
navigated away from unsaved editor

persona_rationale: I tapped away for one second and my title vanished. Apps I use always go 'wait, save first?' This one just eats your work.

suggested_fix.current: Silent navigation away from unsaved editor
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a 'Leave without saving? Your changes will be lost' confirmation prompt
